title: lotodes plicatum
intro: Lotodes plicatum, also known as the lotus tree or lotus-leafed willow, is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are rich in salicylic acid and other compounds, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Lotodes plicatum is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function.
